inline_comments.message:
there are many of these that can be trimmed, ie. openjdk8, bash, nss, procps, etc .. I think they are in policy-common-alpine that this Dockerfile inherits from.

Similarly, iproute2, iputils, openssh .. are not needed for the simulator.
Why not use $POLICY_HOME all across and not PDP_SIM_HOME so it is uniform across images ..
how is this used? message-router is only known in oom environments
Agreed. I was just finishing this part quickly by referring the dmaap simulator. Such refactoring needs to be done.

By specifying the hostname as message-router it could be used directly if the dmaap also comes up with that name. For example, if you look at the integration-csit >scripts>policy>policy-apex-pdp docker-compose file and topic.properties file, I have specified dmaap as the host name there.  In this case, "dmaap" host will be available in that network. Similarly, message-router is specified here so that it could be used directly in case if needed(by specifying the name as "message-router" while bringing up the dmaap simulator). 

Instead, if needed to specify the direct ip or any other host name, we could just mount this topic.properties as well with the known ip address.
